raw_response:
I **agree** with the conclusion reached by @Agent2 regarding option D, Norovirus. One key point not emphasized before was the incubation period often associated with norovirus infections; it usually ranges from 12 hours up to 72 hours, fitting well into our scenario where patients fell ill around three days post-exposure. Additionally, norovirus can survive outside living cells for extended periods under cold conditions, making shellfish especially susceptible carriers if they were improperly stored or handled.
